mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly lifting equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rapid deterioration in monthly employment growth signals an imminent economic contraction, causing investors to price in lower corporate profits and driving equity prices down over the next week.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines by more than 50,000 in a single month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the signal shows no predictive power over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent strength in housing starts and industrial production confirms robust aggregate demand, which supports corporate revenue growth and drives equity prices higher over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a baseline buy-and-hold strategy by at least 1.5% over a 5-year backtest, or the interaction term shows a statistically insignificant coefficient (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in industrial production following a recessionary trough indicates supply-side normalization, which reduces input cost volatility and stabilizes profit margins, leading to positive equity returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain flat or negative during confirmed recovery phases, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50% over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in retail sales volume reflects weakening consumer spending, which directly reduces corporate top-line growth expectations and forces equity valuations lower within a short trading window.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS falls by more than 3% month-over-month
failure_condition: Equity markets rally despite consecutive monthly declines in retail sales, or the signal exhibits a negative rank IC greater than -0.05 over any 12-month period.

mechanism_narrative: Continuous quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP expands the total addressable market for corporate earnings, providing a structural tailwind that lifts equity valuations over a multi-month horizon.
failure_condition: The hypothesis fails if quarterly GDP growth correlates negatively with 60-day forward equity returns across two consecutive business cycles, or the predictive R-squared falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous rises in unemployment and federal funds rates indicate a policy-induced economic slowdown, which compresses equity multiples and drives significant downward pressure on stock prices over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces positive forward returns in more than 55% of observations, or the interaction coefficient fails to reach statistical significance (p > 0.05) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes real purchasing power and forces the central bank to maintain restrictive policy, which increases discount rates and compresses equity valuations over a weekly horizon.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL implies an annualized inflation rate exceeding 3.5%
failure_condition: Equity prices rise consistently during periods of accelerating inflation, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 45% over a 2-year out-of-sample test.
